allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps cultural-dimensions-theory connect local behavior differences to broader coordination patterns and long-term effects.
allies.source_quote: Models are invaluable in systems thinking contexts, helping users gain a big-picture view, recognize patterns, and visualize the dynamics of complex systems.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cultural-dimensions-theory conflicts with optionality when broad cultural branching postpones concrete coordination commitments that now need closure.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cultural-dimensions-theory conflicts with optionality (optionality) when broad cultural branching postpones concrete coordination commitments.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cultural-dimensions-theory conflicts with opportunity-cost when adapting to one norm set hides the outcomes foregone in alternative contexts.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cultural-dimensions-theory conflicts with opportunity-cost (opportunity cost) when adaptation to one norm set masks foregone outcomes in alternative contexts.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved only current-vocabulary first-order seams that were explicit or cleanly warranted by the raw source.
donor_drops: Did not keep donor-shaped or out-of-vocabulary relation surfaces; omission beat weak forcing.
open_questions: Higher-order candidates remain observational only and belong in later selective review rather than in this closure batch.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only for this model.
antagonists: Any broader antagonistic surface not preserved here was omitted because the current first-order vocabulary did not justify forcing it.
structured_tensions: Only explicit or cleanly source-supported tensions that survive current-vocabulary discipline were preserved.
